tokens: Can you see us underneath the LM over at the SEQ bay, Houston?

tokens: We can see your feet sticking out underneath the structure of the LM descent stage.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I'm just on the other side of the...
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Now we can see you (actually Neil)...
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The doors are open, and it looks like they are going to stay up without any problem.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: We are about to lose you on the Omnis.
Request high-gain antenna, REACQ mode; pitch 20, yaw 135.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You want to pick an area, Neil?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Columbia, yaw 175 on the high gain.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Columbia's locked up on the high gain, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Collins

tokens: And (on) the manual deployment of the LR cubed, the little spring that is at the end of the string pulled off of the pip pin.
However, I was able to reach up and get hold of the pip pin and pull it loose.
So, it will be deployed manually, also.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I'm at about the LM 7:30 position (SE) at about 60 feet.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: And the doors are closed and locked.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: (To Neil) Okay; have you got us a good area picked out?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Well, I think right out on that rise out there is probably as good as any.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Let's probably stay on the high ground there and...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: The edge of that crater is really soft.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Yeah; that's real soft there, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: (To Neil) Get a couple of close-ups on these quite rounded, large boulders.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: About 40 feet out; I'd say out at the end of that next...
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Well, it's going to be a little difficult to find a good level spot here.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: The top of that next little ridge there.
Wouldn't that be a pretty good place?
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Okay; how about I put the LR-cubed right about here?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I'm going to have to get on the other side of this rock here.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I would go right around that crater to the left there.
Isn't that a level spot there?
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: I think this right here is just as level.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: (Garbled) boulders look like basalt, and they have probably two percent white minerals in them - white crystals.
And the thing that I reported as the vesicular before, I don't believe I believe that any more.
I think it's small craters; they look like little impact craters where
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: I have the seismic experiment flipped over now, and I'm aligning it with the Sun; (but) I'm having a little bit of difficulty getting the BB (to) go in the center.
It wants to move around and around on the outside.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I say I'm not having too much success in leveling the PSE experiment.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: The laser reflector is (pause) installed and the bubble is level and the alignment appears to be good.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: (To Neil) Okay, you want to take a look at this BB and see what you make out of it?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I found it pretty hard to get perfectly level, too.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: It won't go on the inside.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: That little cup is convex now, instead of concave.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Houston, I don't think there's any hope for using this leveling device to come up with an accurate level.
It looks to me as though the cup here, that the BB is in, is now convex instead of concave.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: If you think it looks level by eyeball, go ahead.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Houston, as I was facing the PSE (Passive Seismic Experiment), the right-hand solar array deployed automatically.
The left-hand I had to manually (garbled) restraining bar at the far end.
And all parts of the solar array are clear of the ground now.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I understand that you did successfully deploy both solar arrays.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Pause) And there isn't any way of telling whether that's lined up, without getting in the way; maybe I can get down here.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Well, that appears to be pointing (garbled) (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: We've been looking at your consumables, and you're in good shape.
Subject to your concurrence, we'd like to extend the duration of the EVA one-five (15) minutes from nominal.
We will still give Buzz a hack at 10 minutes prior, for heading in.
Your current elapsed time is 2 plus 12.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: If you're still in the vicinity of the PSE, could you get a photograph of the ball level?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We'll get a photograph of that.
Houston, what time would you estimate we should allow for the
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Would you believe the ball is right in the middle now?
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: Take a picture before it moves.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: (Responding to the question Buzz asked at 111:09:18, making a mis-identification) Neil, this is Houston.
We're estimating about 10 minutes for the documented sampling.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Like you to terminate charging battery Bravo at 111 plus 15.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You've got about 10 minutes left now prior to commencing your EVA termination activities.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The passive seismic experiment has been uncaged and we're observing short-period oscillations in it.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (To Houston) I hope you're watching how hard I have to hit this into the ground, to the tune of about 5 inches, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Yeah, you caught the cable again.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: (Garbled under Bruce) turn to the right.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: We'd like you all to get two core tubes and the solar wind experiment; two core tubes and the solar wind.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: While I'm getting the next one (the second core), maybe you can (garbled) the box a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Okay; I'll take care of it.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: You have approximately 3 minutes until you must commence your EVA termination activities.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, do you plan on commencing your sleep on the backside this pass?
If so, we'll disable uplink to you while we're talking to the LM.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Houston, were you able to record, in a documentary way (with the TV), where the two core tube samples were taken?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: I didn't get a stereopair of those two, but they are right in the vicinity of the solar wind.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: After you've got the core tubes and the solar wind, anything else that you can throw into the box would be acceptable.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, you want to pick up some stuff, and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: It's about time for you to start your EVA closeout activities.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Neil and Buzz, this is Houston.
We'd like to remind you of the close-up (Gold) camera magazine before you start up the ladder, Buzz.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Got that (meaning the ALSCC) over with you, Neil?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: No, the close-up camera's underneath the MESA.
I'll have to pick it up with the tongs.
(Just off-camera at the right) I'm picking up several pieces of really vesicular rock out here, now.
transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: You didn't get anything in those environmental samples, did you?
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Well, I don't think we'll have time.
transcript.speaker: Aldrin

tokens: Let's press on with getting the close-up camera magazine and closing out of the sample return container.

tokens: Can you quickly stick this (film magazine) in my pocket, Neil...

tokens: ...and I'll head on up the ladder?

tokens: Anything more before I head on up, Bruce?

tokens: Head on up the ladder, Buzz.

tokens: (Long Pause) Did you get that solar wind rolled up there, Buzz?

tokens: If you think you can reach this hook that's hanging over here?
You might entertain the idea of sending up the second one (that is, the second rock box) that way.

tokens: Get the film off of the (Hasselblad)...

tokens: (Pause) Pick that up (that is, take care of the task) now.

transcript.speaker: Armstrong

tokens: (Pause) All right; easy in the hatch now.
